segments.characterFr: Bonjour, bienvenue au Café du Coin. Qu'est-ce que je vous sers aujourd'hui ?
segments.characterEn: Hello, welcome to Café du Coin. What can I get for you today?
segments.fr: Je voudrais un café au lait et un croissant, s'il vous plaît.
segments.en: I would like a café au lait and a croissant, please.
segments.grammarNote: 'Je voudrais' is the polite conditional form for 'I would like', softer than 'je veux'.
segments.pronunciationTip: Link 'un café' smoothly, the 'n' carries into 'café'.

segments.characterFr: Très bien. C'est pour ici ou à emporter ?
segments.characterEn: Very well. Is that for here or to take away?
segments.en: It's for here, thank you.
segments.grammarNote: 'Pour ici' and 'à emporter' are the two fixed café phrases for 'for here' and 'to go'.

segments.characterFr: D'accord. Ça fait sept euros cinquante.
segments.characterEn: Alright. That comes to seven fifty.
segments.en: Here you go, keep the change.
segments.grammarNote: 'Gardez' is the polite imperative ('vous' form) of 'garder', to keep.

segments.characterFr: Merci beaucoup, c'est très gentil !
segments.characterEn: Thank you very much, that's very kind!
segments.en: You're welcome, have a good day!
segments.grammarNote: 'De rien' literally means 'of nothing', the standard reply to thanks.

segments.characterFr: Bonjour, je peux vous aider ?
segments.characterEn: Hello, can I help you?
segments.fr: Oui, un billet pour Lyon, s'il vous plaît.
segments.en: Yes, one ticket for Lyon, please.
segments.grammarNote: 'Un billet pour' + destination is the standard phrase for buying a ticket.

segments.characterFr: Aller simple ou aller-retour ?
segments.characterEn: One-way or round trip?
segments.en: Round trip, for this weekend.
segments.grammarNote: 'Aller-retour' is a fixed compound noun, always used together.

segments.characterFr: Le train part à quatorze heures, quai numéro trois.
segments.characterEn: The train leaves at 2 pm, platform number three.
segments.en: Okay, thank you. Is it direct?
segments.grammarNote: 24-hour time ('quatorze heures') is standard in French for schedules.

segments.characterFr: Oui, c'est direct, deux heures de trajet.
segments.characterEn: Yes, it's direct, a two-hour trip.
segments.en: Perfect, here you go for the ticket.
segments.grammarNote: 'Voilà' introduces something being handed over or pointed out.

segments.characterFr: Bonjour, votre passeport et votre billet, s'il vous plaît.
segments.characterEn: Hello, your passport and ticket, please.
segments.fr: Voici mon passeport et ma réservation.
segments.en: Here's my passport and my reservation.
segments.grammarNote: 'Voici' points to something close at hand, like 'here is'.

segments.characterFr: Merci. Vous avez des bagages à enregistrer ?
segments.characterEn: Thank you. Do you have any bags to check?
segments.en: Yes, just one suitcase.
segments.grammarNote: 'À enregistrer' is 'to check in' (luggage), infinitive after 'à'.

segments.characterFr: Très bien, posez-la ici, s'il vous plaît.
segments.characterEn: Very well, place it here, please.
segments.en: Okay, here it is.
segments.grammarNote: 'Posez-la' is an imperative with an attached pronoun ('it').

segments.characterFr: Votre porte d'embarquement est la douze, embarquement à seize heures.
segments.characterEn: Your boarding gate is twelve, boarding at 4 pm.
segments.fr: Merci beaucoup, bonne journée à vous aussi.
segments.en: Thank you very much, good day to you too.
segments.grammarNote: 'À vous aussi' returns a wish politely, mirroring what was said.

segments.characterFr: Bonjour, je peux vous aider ? Vous semblez perdu.
segments.characterEn: Hello, can I help you? You seem lost.
segments.fr: Oui, merci. Je cherche la bibliothèque municipale.
segments.en: Yes, thank you. I'm looking for the municipal library.
segments.grammarNote: 'Je cherche' + noun is the direct way to say 'I'm looking for'.

segments.characterFr: Ah, c'est facile. Continuez tout droit, puis tournez à gauche.
segments.characterEn: Ah, that's easy. Keep going straight, then turn left.
segments.fr: Tout droit, puis à gauche, d'accord.
segments.en: Straight ahead, then left, okay.
segments.grammarNote: 'Tout droit' means 'straight ahead'; don't confuse with 'à droite' (to the right).

segments.characterFr: C'est à environ cinq minutes à pied, juste après la boulangerie.
segments.characterEn: It's about a five-minute walk, just past the bakery.
segments.en: Perfect, is it far from here?
segments.grammarNote: 'À pied' means 'on foot', a common way to describe walking distance.

segments.characterFr: Non, pas du tout, c'est tout près.
segments.characterEn: No, not at all, it's very close.
segments.en: Thank you so much for your help!
segments.grammarNote: 'Merci infiniment' is a stronger, warmer way to say 'thank you very much'.

segments.characterFr: Bonjour, je peux vous renseigner ?
segments.characterEn: Hello, can I help you with anything?
segments.fr: Oui, je cherche un pull en taille moyenne.
segments.en: Yes, I'm looking for a sweater in medium size.
segments.grammarNote: 'En taille' + size word describes clothing size.

segments.characterFr: Nous en avons en bleu et en gris, vous préférez quelle couleur ?
segments.characterEn: We have them in blue and grey, which colour do you prefer?
segments.en: I really like the blue one.
segments.grammarNote: 'Me plaît' means 'pleases me', the standard way to say you like something.

segments.characterFr: Voici, la cabine d'essayage est juste là.
segments.characterEn: Here you go, the fitting room is right there.
segments.en: Thank you, I'll try it on.
segments.grammarNote: 'Je vais' + infinitive expresses the near future, 'I'm going to'.

segments.characterFr: Alors, ça vous va bien ?
segments.characterEn: So, does it fit you well?
segments.fr: Oui, ça me va parfaitement, je le prends.
segments.en: Yes, it fits me perfectly, I'll take it.
segments.grammarNote: 'Ça va' + indirect pronoun describes fit, not just general wellbeing.

segments.characterFr: Bonsoir, vous avez réservé une table ?
segments.characterEn: Good evening, do you have a table reservation?
segments.fr: Oui, au nom de Dubois, pour deux personnes.
segments.en: Yes, under the name Dubois, for two people.
segments.grammarNote: 'Au nom de' means 'under the name of', standard for reservations.

segments.characterFr: Parfait, suivez-moi. Voici le menu du jour.
segments.characterEn: Perfect, follow me. Here's today's menu.
segments.en: Thank you, what do you recommend?
segments.grammarNote: 'Qu'est-ce que' is the full form for asking 'what', common in spoken French.

segments.characterFr: Le poisson du jour est excellent ce soir.
segments.characterEn: The fish of the day is excellent tonight.
segments.fr: D'accord, je vais prendre ça, avec de l'eau, s'il vous plaît.
segments.en: Okay, I'll have that, with water, please.
segments.grammarNote: 'Je vais prendre' is the near-future way to order, softer than the plain present.

segments.characterFr: Excellent choix. Ce sera prêt dans quinze minutes.
segments.characterEn: Excellent choice. It will be ready in fifteen minutes.
segments.en: Perfect, thank you very much.
segments.grammarNote: 'Ce sera' is the future tense of 'c'est', meaning 'it will be'.
